'''Sobo Leeda''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Ualaq/Legends|Ualaq]] [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] who lived during the last [[Standard year/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. By [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] Leeda lived on the [[Outland Transit Station]] in the [[Baxel sector/Legends|Baxel sector]], where he stole fuel cells from the stations [[Toydarian/Legends|Toydarian]] administrator [[Rozatta]]. The loss of the fuel cells cost the Toydarian greatly and she placed a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] of 3,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for his capture alive, offering only half that if he was brought in dead. The [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] encountered Leeda in Outland Station's docking facilities whilst the hunter was on the station chasing the [[Muttani]] criminal [[Meeko Ghintee]]. Leeda attacked Fett, but the hunter had been informed of the price on the Aqualish's head by Rozatta herself and he captured the criminal alive, claiming the bounty.

==Behind the scenes==
Sobo Leeda first appeared in the video game ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'', which was released by [[LucasArts]] in [[2002]]. Leeda was one of several secondary bounties that players could optionally capture on the third level of the games first chapter. Players could capture Leeda alive or dead, or not capture him at all however this article assumes that Fett captured him alive as this is the highest paying option.
